current_r_source: Endler 1983 predator-on population reduced mating success in high-predation environment
F2_endorsement: F2 confirmed: females prefer more ornamented males even in high-predation zones
F3_evidence: M4: highly ornamented males die earlier; ornament persists via female choice
F4_feedback_failure: no negative signal fed back to female preference gene; preference and ornament coevolve positively
notes: Route A: signal calibrated for low-predation ancestral env; high-predation = mismatch

current_r_source: Heinsohn et al. 2019: females holding superabundant artificial nest boxes show reduced fledgling count vs wild
F2_endorsement: F2: females preferentially defend high-tenure nests even when correlated with lower fledgling success
F3_evidence: M3: trans-generational norm; daughters of nest-holders preferentially occupy boxes
F4_feedback_failure: nest box provisioning program lacks corrective feedback loop; high occupancy rate obscures per-nest productivity
notes: Route A: ancestral signal (nest stability) valid in natural cavity scarcity; artificial abundance = mismatch

current_r_source: Takahashi et al. 2008: eyespot count uncorrelated with male condition or offspring fitness in wild population

notes: Route B: eyespot supernormal signal; no ancestral-environment referent beyond basic symmetry detection
